React is used to build single-page applications. React allows us to create reusable UI components. Start learning React now ❯ 🏁 Tip: Sign in to track your progress. Learning by Examples Our "Show React" tool makes it easy to demonstrate React. It shows both the code and the result. Example: import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'; function Hello() { return (

Hello World!

); } createRoot(document.getElementById('root')).render( ); Run Example » Learning by Exercises Many chapters in this tutorial end with an exercise where you can check you level of knowledge. You can study at W3Schools without creating an account. What You Should Already Know Before you continue you should have a basic understanding of the following: HTML CSS JavaScript If you want to study these subjects first, find the tutorials on our Home page . In addition, you should also have some experience with the new JavaScript features introduced in ECMAScript 6 (ES6), you will learn about them in the React ES6 chapter.
